title: EU Tech Watchdog Eyes Volta-LuminCore Merger
body:
European competition authorities have opened a preliminary review of Volta Systems' proposed acquisition of LuminCore, the Dutch photonics company, according to two people familiar with the matter.

The European Commission's Directorate-General for Competition is examining whether the deal could foreclose rivals' access to specialist chip components, particularly in the nascent quantum computing supply chain. A formal investigation has not been launched, and the review is at an early stage.

The Berlin startup announced the $180 million purchase of LuminCore last week. Volta has significant market share in the fault-tolerant quantum processor segment in Europe, and regulators are said to be concerned about vertical integration foreclosing smaller players.

A Volta spokesperson said the company "welcomes regulatory engagement" and is confident the transaction will be approved. LuminCore's co-founder, Pieter van der Berg, declined to comment.

Analysts noted the review is consistent with a broader EU posture of scrutinizing tech acquisitions more aggressively, even at the startup stage. One Brussels-based lawyer described the move as "a shot across the bow, not a blockade."
